question:
Over $300M committed to the Monad public sale?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if total commitments for the Monad raise on Coinbase exceeds the number specified in the title before the raise closes. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is the "Total requested" value displayed on the Coinbase raise page available at https://www.coinbase.com/token-sales/monad.

If the final commitment amount cannot be verified by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, or if relevant information becomes permanently unavailable within the market timeframe, this market will resolve to “No.”

evidences.title: Monad Labs raises $225M for Ethereum competitor layer-1 ...
evidences.content: UPDATED 13:40 EDT / APRIL 09 2024 ### Monad Labs raises $225M for Ethereum competitor layer-1 blockchain by Kyt Dotson Monad Labs Inc., the maker of the Ethereum-compatible blockchain Monad, today announced that it has raised $225 million in new funding led by Paradigm with the aim to accelerate the development of its scalable network capable of high transaction speeds. Other investors joining the round included Electric Capital, GreenOaks, Castle Island Ventures, Animoca Ventures, Lightspeed Faction, Coinbase Ventures, Wintermute Ventures and numerous others. This round is one of the largest seen so far this year for a crypto firm, showing a significant financial contribution for Monad, which was reported to be in talks with Paradigm for the raise early last month.

evidences.title: Complete Guide to the Upcoming Public Token Sale on Coinbase
evidences.content: ## Overview: What Is Monad? Monad is a high-throughput, EVM-compatible blockchain designed to deliver over 10,000 transactions per second (TPS) with low transaction fees and rapid finality of around 0.8 seconds. The project’s mission is to tackle the scalability challenges seen in leading networks like Ethereum while ensuring decentralization and providing widespread access for both users and developers. Since its founding in 2022, Monad, operated by Category Labs, has raised $225 million from notable backers such as Paradigm, Dragonfly Capital, Electric Capital, and Castle Island Ventures. The network is set to launch with 200 globally distributed validators running on consumer-grade hardware to further lower the barriers to entry. ## Public MON Token Sale: Key Dates and Details [...] A welcome pack worth 6200 USDT for new users! Sign up now! Monad, a next-generation Layer-1 blockchain, is preparing for the highly anticipated launch of its native MON token, with a public sale taking place on Coinbase’s newly launched Token Sales platform. Backed by prominent investors and built by Category Labs, Monad is positioning itself as a high-performance, Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)-c...

evidences.title: monad / mon token – coinbase token sales disclosure1
evidences.content: Sale: up to 7.5B MON (7.5% of initial total supply). Sale Price: $0.025 per MON. Implied Fully Diluted Value (FDV) of the Monad Network: $2.5 billion. 2 Determination of the Sale Price: Prior to the public sale on Coinbase token sales, there has been no public market for MON tokens. The price of $0.025 per MON token in the public sale is a fixed price based on a $2.5 billion fully diluted network valuation and an initial total supply of 100 billion MON tokens. The sale price was determined through a structured approach that considers several key factors, including market conditions, competitive positioning, and strategic objectives. This methodology ensures that the sale price reflects both the intrinsic value of the Monad network and broader market dynamics. The pricing strategy aims to [...] and broader market dynamics. The pricing strategy aims to establish a fair market entry point for retail purchasers, balancing initial demand and long-term value appreciation. Jurisdictions: Participation in the public sale is available to eligible purchasers in the following jurisdictions where Coinbase operates: Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Angola, Argentina, Armenia, Aruba, Azerbaijan, Ba...

evidences.content: 1. Home 2. News 3. Blockchains # Monad Labs Raises $225M in This Year’s Biggest Funding Round The round was led by Paradigm, and includes MakerDAO’s Rune Christensen and Coinbase Ventures. By:Pedro Solimano • Blockchains an aerial view of a highway with cars moving at high speeds, depicted in a minimalistic style with vivid neon colors Crypto VCs are still vying to back the leading Layer 1 chain , as Monad Labs closes a whopping $225 million round. Led by Paradigm, the round includes Coinbase Ventures, Castle Island Ventures, Electric Capital, and a number of angel investors such as MakerDAO’s Rune Christensen, professional NFL player Saquon Barkley, and Reflexivity Research’s Will Clemente.

evidences.title: Monad's Mission: Why Monad?
evidences.content: MON is the Monad network’s native token, used to pay gas fees, secure the chain via staking, and align validators, developers, and users around the growth of the protocol. At the launch of Monad Public Mainnet, the total initial supply of MON will be 100 billion tokens. Up to 7.5B $MON tokens (7.5% of initial token supply) will be made available to the public for purchase in the public sale at a price of $0.025 per token. The Coinbase token sales platform accommodates a minimum bid size of $100 and a maximum bid size of $100,000 (subject to higher maximum thresholds for Coinbase One subscribers pursuant to the Coinbase token sale platform terms). [...] ## Public sale on Coinbase token sales platform Decentralization is only possible with widely distributed ownership of the MON token. A healthy ecosystem consists not only of developers and users, but also tokenholders. We are pleased to share that Coinbase has provided a platform for the Monad Foundation to distribute MON through a public sale by its subsidiary, MF Services (BVI), Ltd. The Coinbase token sales platform brings public sales to retail purchasers in the US and globally, allowing for smaller allocations across more pa...

evidences.title: Blockchain Developer Monad Labs Raises $225M Led by Paradigm
evidences.content: Ethereum's speed, or lack of it, has spurred many efforts to provide a faster DeFi environment, including alternative layer-1 blockchains such as Solana and Cardano and layer-2 chains that bundle, or roll up, transactions and process them at a faster pace before sending them back to Ethereum for validation. Monad said it rewrote the blockchain code, introducing techniques such as parallel execution to optimize performance at all levels of the protocol, to improve throughput. Other investors in the round included Electric Capital, Castle Island Ventures, Animoca Ventures, Coinbase Ventures and CoinFund. Read more: Crypto Startup Monad Labs Aims to Create Next 'Ethereum Killer' After Raising $19M Terms and Privacy Policy Privacy dashboard ## Recommended stories [...] Sheldon Reback 1 min read Monad Labs raised $225 million in a funding round led by Paradigm. The money will help in the development of its faster version of the Ethereum blockchain that is compatible with existing DeFi applications. Monad Labs, which is looking to develop a faster version of the Ethereum blockchain, said it raised $225 million in a funding round led by Paradigm that will allow it to add staff and spen...

evidences.title: Monad Token Sale Goes Live on Coinbase Exchange
evidences.content: ### What's the Scoop? How It Works: Until November 22, Coinbase Coinbase users can commit USDC to purchase MON directly from the Monad Foundation via Coinbase's Monad token sale page. For the Monad token sale, users are able to commit a minimum of 100 USDC and a maximum of 100,000 USDC. Wide Allocation: To maximize the number of individuals who receive MON tokens, Coinbase will fill the smallest order sizes first in the event of an oversubscribed sale. This mechanism is known as “filling up from the bottom," and means that large orders may have a portion of their USDC refunded. ### What's the Take? evidences.content: But the lack of regulation meant ICOs were mired in hundreds of dubious projects, if not outright scams. They were swiftly cracked down on by the US Securities and Exchange Commission. Last month, rival blockchain project MegaETH sold $50 million worth of its native token at a $1 billion valuation through Sonar, a token sale venue tied to early-stage investing platform Echo. evidences.title: Get early access to upcoming tokens - Coinbase
evidences.content: Example: In a sale with 1000 tokens available, three participants (Low, Middle, High) request 100, 500, and 1000 tokens respectively. All participants are first allocated 100 tokens, leaving 700 tokens remaining and 1300 in unfilled demand from Middle and High requests -- Low's request has been completely filled. The remaining 700 tokens are split evenly between Middle and High's because they both have unfilled requests (400 and 900 respectively). Middle and High receive 350 tokens in the second round of distribution, each receiving a total of 450 tokens. ## Help Center If you need more support please visit our help center or check out the FAQs. [...] If the total number of requested tokens is greater than the total available tokens, a distribution mechanism, called “filling up from the bottom,” attempts to create a broad distribution among sale participants and limit asset concentration among large buyers. The mechanism first identifies the user with the smallest unfilled amount (“the bottom”) and grants the specified amount to all requestors. The system then identifies the next smallest unfilled amount and grants that amount to all requestors. The system continues fulfilling r...

evidences.title: Coinbase Acquires Echo for ~$375M - insights4vc
evidences.content: This move echoes initiatives by other platforms. Binance pioneered exchange-hosted token launches in the last cycle. CoinList and Republic facilitated token sales with U.S. investor compliance. Firms such as Galaxy Digital and 21.co have developed distribution channels for new crypto assets. Coinbase’s entry into primary offerings raises competitive stakes, but also risks. It will need to manage potential conflicts of interest, for example avoiding any implicit guarantee that a token raised via Echo will later be listed on Coinbase’s exchange. The company plans to keep Echo as a standalone platform for now and only integrate Sonar directly, partly to maintain a neutral listings process. [...] 2023–2024: Transitioned from commentator to builder by founding Echo in 2023. Echo operated as a non-custodial platform that enabled vetted communities to invest in early-stage crypto projects. By mid-2025 it facilitated more than two hundred million dollars of funding through private sales and a public launchpad called Sonar. Notable raises included MegaETH, Initia, and Plasma. 2025: Sold Echo to Coinbase in late 2025. Accepted an advisory role at Paradigm, a leading crypto venture firm, w...

evidences.title: Coinbase to buy Echo in $375 million crypto platform deal | LinkedIn
evidences.content: Echo’s platform allows projects to raise capital via private sales or public token sales using its Sonar product, empowering communities to invest alongside institutional players. Since launch, Echo has facilitated over $200 million in fundraising across nearly 300 deals, becoming a key player in community-led finance. [...] By integrating Echo’s tools, Coinbase will offer a full-stack solution for token issuers—from creation and fundraising to trading and liquidity—building on its earlier acquisition of Liquifi (acq. Coinbase), which focuses on token management and cap tables. The move will initially support crypto token sales, with plans to expand to tokenized securities and real-world assets, driving the evolution of transparent, globally accessible, and community-aligned capital markets onchain. The announce on #Coinbase website in the first comment. evidences.title: Token sales | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: • If you no longer want the token, wait for distribution, then sell or convert it. • Selling a token shortly after purchase ("flipping") may result in smaller allocations in future sales. Allocation details• If total requests exceed available supply, allocations are determined using a “fill-from-the-bottom” approach to promote broad distribution. • You may receive fewer tokens than requested, but will only pay for tokens received. • Any unused USDC is returned to your balance. Allocation limits• Vary from sale to sale (see token sale page for details) Taxes• Taxes on token purchases are based on the fixed purchase price. • US customers may receive an IRS Form 1099-DA for buying or selling tokens. evidences.title: Monad token sale and mainnet launch overview
evidences.content: Monad is an Ethereum-compatible layer-1 blockchain conducting a public token sale on Coinbase. The sale offers 7.5 billion MON tokens (7.5% of total supply) at $0.025 per token, implying a fully diluted valuation of roughly $2.5 billion if fully subscribed. The sale is hosted on Coinbase’s regulated token-launch platform and includes participation rules such as a $100 minimum bid, a $100,000 maximum bid, and an allocation algorithm designed to prioritize smaller bids.
